question: The cross-education effect in training refers to
options: Both aerobic and anaerobic fitness in shorter sessions
Training one limb can improve strength in the untrained limb
The smallest training stimulus that produces measurable adaptation
Motion capture and force plates to improve technique
answer: Training one limb can improve strength in the untrained limb

question: Satellite cells in muscle tissue are responsible for
options: The energy cost of physical activities relative to rest
Matching carbohydrate intake to training intensity and volume
Muscle repair and growth after damage
Guides athlete development from childhood through to elite performance
answer: Muscle repair and growth after damage

question: The concept of relative energy deficiency in sport (RED-S)
options: It activates the exact muscles and movement patterns used in the sport
Both aerobic and anaerobic fitness in shorter sessions
Occurs when energy intake is insufficient relative to training demands
The energy cost of physical activities relative to rest
answer: Occurs when energy intake is insufficient relative to training demands

answer: Matching carbohydrate intake to training intensity and volume
